intro:
Body fat percentage is one of the most meaningful metrics for assessing fitness and health. Unlike BMI, which only considers height and weight, body fat percentage tells you exactly how much of your body is fat versus lean mass (muscle, bone, water, and organs). Knowing your body fat percentage helps you set realistic fitness goals, track progress, and understand your metabolic health.

Our calculator uses the U.S. Navy circumference method, one of the most accurate field measurement techniques available without specialized equipment. For men, it requires waist and neck circumference alongside height. For women, it adds hip circumference to account for the natural difference in fat distribution between genders.

Healthy body fat ranges vary by gender and age. Most health authorities consider 10–20% healthy for men and 18–28% healthy for women, though athletes often fall well below these ranges.
howItWorks: Select your gender, then enter your height, neck circumference, and waist circumference (measured at the navel) in centimeters. Women also enter hip circumference at the widest point. The calculator applies the U.S. Navy formula to estimate your body fat percentage, fat mass, and lean mass in kilograms.
interpretationGuide: Essential fat (the minimum needed for organ function) is 2–5% for men and 10–13% for women. Athletes typically range from 6–13% (men) or 14–20% (women). Fitness ranges are 14–17% (men) and 21–24% (women). Acceptable ranges up to 24% (men) and 31% (women) are within healthy bounds. Above those thresholds indicates excess fat that may increase health risks.

faqs.question: How accurate is the Navy body fat method?
faqs.answer: The U.S. Navy circumference method has an error margin of about ±3–4% compared to DEXA scan measurements. It is more accurate than BMI for assessing body composition but less precise than hydrostatic weighing or DEXA.

faqs.question: What is a healthy body fat percentage for men?
faqs.answer: For men, 10–20% body fat is generally considered healthy. Athletes may be 6–13%, fitness enthusiasts 14–17%, and acceptable range extends to about 24%. Above 25% is often classified as overfat.

faqs.question: What is a healthy body fat percentage for women?
faqs.answer: Women naturally carry more essential fat than men. Healthy ranges for women are typically 18–28%. Athlete levels are around 14–20%, fitness levels 21–24%, and acceptable up to 31%. Above 32% may indicate excess fat.

faqs.question: Where exactly should I measure my waist?
faqs.answer: For the Navy method, men measure their waist at the navel (belly button level). Women also measure at the navel. This is different from the smallest waist measurement used in fashion — use the navel as your consistent landmark.

faqs.question: How do I measure my neck circumference?
faqs.answer: Measure just below the larynx (Adam's apple) with your head level. Keep the tape snug but not compressing the skin. Round up to the nearest 0.5 cm for best accuracy.

faqs.question: Why does the formula differ for men and women?
faqs.answer: Men and women distribute body fat differently. Women tend to store more fat in the hips and thighs, which is why the female formula includes hip circumference. The different formulas account for these sex-specific fat distribution patterns.

faqs.question: Can I use this calculator to track fat loss progress?
faqs.answer: Yes, circumference measurements change as you lose fat, making this an effective free method to track progress over time. Take measurements at the same time of day and under the same conditions for consistency.

faqs.question: What is lean body mass?
faqs.answer: Lean body mass is everything in your body that is not fat: muscle, bone, water, organs, and connective tissue. It determines your basal metabolic rate — higher lean mass means a higher resting calorie burn.

faqs.question: How is this different from BMI?
faqs.answer: BMI uses only height and weight and cannot distinguish between fat and muscle. Body fat percentage directly estimates how much of your body weight is fat. A muscular person can have a high BMI but a low body fat percentage.

faqs.question: What are the other methods for measuring body fat?
faqs.answer: More precise methods include DEXA (dual-energy X-ray absorptiometry) scan, hydrostatic weighing, Bod Pod (air displacement plethysmography), and bioelectrical impedance analysis (BIA). These range from moderately to highly accurate but require equipment or facilities.
